summary: First Firebase integration test (iteration 15). Tested all 12 required flows. Two critical blockers found: (1) Firestore database does not exist for Firebase project iwhistle-6f5d1 — ALL authenticated flows are broken because user profiles cannot be loaded. (2) Referee demo password mismatch: Login.jsx had 'referee123' but Firebase Auth account uses 'Referee123' (capital R). Fixed #2 in code. #1 requires Firebase Console action by main agent.

integration_issues.flow: All login flows (referee, manager, new user registration)
integration_issues.issue: Firestore database '(default)' does not exist for project iwhistle-6f5d1. After Firebase Auth succeeds, getOrCreateProfile() calls getDoc()/setDoc() which fail with 404. App gets stuck in infinite loading spinner. Error: 'The database (default) does not exist for project iwhistle-6f5d1'

integration_issues.flow: Demo Referee login (Try Demo Account → Log in as Referee)
integration_issues.issue: Password mismatch: Login.jsx sent 'referee123' but Firebase Auth account uses 'Referee123' (capital R). FIXED in this iteration — changed to 'Referee123' in Login.jsx line 53.

issues: None of the form inputs have data-testid attributes — reduces testability. All Register.jsx inputs (name, email, phone, password, confirmPassword, submit button) missing data-testid.

test_results.TEST_1_login_page_loads: PASS — /login loads without crash, form visible, all elements present
test_results.TEST_2_referee_login_email_form: FAIL — 'referee123' triggers INVALID_LOGIN_CREDENTIALS (Firebase password is 'Referee123'). Code fixed in this iteration.
test_results.TEST_3_dashboard_with_user_name: BLOCKED — Firestore not created; cannot login to reach dashboard
test_results.TEST_4_manager_login: FAIL — manager@demo.com/manager123 passes Firebase Auth but Firestore returns 404, app stuck in loading spinner
test_results.TEST_7_wrong_password_error: PASS — Login Failed toast shown correctly with 'Invalid email or password.' message; no crash
test_results.TEST_8_logout: BLOCKED — cannot login to test logout
test_results.TEST_9_register_page: PASS (page loads) — Register form at /register loads with all fields. Submit PARTIALLY WORKS: Firebase Auth creates the account but setDoc to Firestore fails silently, app stays on loading spinner
test_results.EXTRA_protected_route_redirect: PASS — /dashboard, /manager etc redirect to /login when unauthenticated

firebase_auth_verification.manager_account: EXISTS — manager@demo.com / manager123 verified via Firebase Auth REST API
firebase_auth_verification.referee_account: EXISTS — referee@demo.com / Referee123 (capital R) verified via Firebase Auth REST API
firebase_auth_verification.firestore_db: DOES NOT EXIST — HTTP 404: 'The database (default) does not exist for project iwhistle-6f5d1. Please visit https://console.cloud.google.com/datastore/setup?project=iwhistle-6f5d1'

action_items: CRITICAL: Create Firestore database for Firebase project iwhistle-6f5d1. Go to: https://console.cloud.google.com/datastore/setup?project=iwhistle-6f5d1 and create a Firestore database in Native mode. Without this, ALL authenticated flows are blocked.
CRITICAL: Apply Firestore security rules from /app/memory/firestore_security_rules.txt after creating the database, OR temporarily set to 'test mode' (allow all reads/writes) for initial testing.
DONE (fixed in this iteration): Referee demo password in Login.jsx was 'referee123' (lowercase), changed to 'Referee123' to match Firebase Auth account.
OPTIONAL: Add data-testid attributes to Register page form inputs for better testability.
After Firestore is created and rules are set, re-run full test suite to verify: login flows, dashboard, seed data, logout, games/calendar/schedule pages.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.getOrCreateProfile: When Firestore fails, the error is caught and setUser(null) is called but no user-facing error is shown from onAuthStateChanged. The login() function does show a toast, but the onAuthStateChanged failure is silent. Consider adding a toast in the onAuthStateChanged catch block.

App gets stuck in loading spinner when Firestore is down — no timeout or fallback error. The onAuthStateChanged handler retries Firestore for ~9 seconds before failing. Consider adding an explicit timeout or error boundary for Firestore connectivity failures.
Register page form inputs lack data-testid attributes — impacts automated testing.
Register.jsx line 29: 'if (user) return <Navigate to='/' replace />' redirects registered+logged users to '/' not '/dashboard'. Should be '/dashboard' for consistency.

success_rate.frontend: 25% (3/12 flows passing: login page loads, wrong password error, register page loads, protected route redirects)

test_credentials.referee: referee@demo.com / Referee123 (capital R — note: Login.jsx now fixed to use correct password)

seed_data_creation: None — blocked by Firestore not existing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Firebase Auth works: manager@demo.com/manager123 and referee@demo.com/Referee123 are confirmed valid in Firebase Auth REST API. BLOCKING ISSUE: Firestore database does not exist for project iwhistle-6f5d1 — must be created in Firebase Console before any Firestore reads/writes work. Once Firestore is created, test: login as both demo users (referee FIRST, then manager to trigger seed), verify dashboard loads, check games/calendar/schedule pages, test logout. The referee password fix (referee123 → Referee123) was applied in Login.jsx in this iteration.

issue_1_firestore_missing.root_cause: Firebase Firestore database was never created for project iwhistle-6f5d1. Firebase Auth and Firestore are separate services — creating a Firebase project does NOT automatically create a Firestore database.
issue_1_firestore_missing.evidence: REST API call to https://firestore.googleapis.com/v1/projects/iwhistle-6f5d1/databases/(default) returns HTTP 404: 'The database (default) does not exist for project iwhistle-6f5d1'
issue_1_firestore_missing.mitigation: Go to Firebase Console → Project iwhistle-6f5d1 → Firestore Database → Create database → Choose Native mode → Select a region. Then apply security rules from /app/memory/firestore_security_rules.txt

issue_2_referee_password.root_cause: Case mismatch: The Firebase Auth account for referee@demo.com was created with password 'Referee123' (capital R), but Login.jsx hardcoded 'referee123' (lowercase r). Firebase Auth passwords are case-sensitive.
issue_2_referee_password.evidence: Tested via Firebase Auth REST API: 'referee123' → INVALID_LOGIN_CREDENTIALS, 'Referee123' → success with idToken
issue_2_referee_password.mitigation: FIXED: Changed Login.jsx line 53 from 'referee123' to 'Referee123'
